description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern of alternating raised and recessed sections. The grooves are deep and sharply defined, giving the block a visually striking, industrial appearance. The sides of the block are solid and smooth, with a consistent dark coloration, while the top surface reveals a lighter, speckled stone texture between the grooves. The block appears dense and heavy, likely constructed from a durable stone material such as granite or concrete. Its design suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly for traffic control or as a decorative element in public spaces. There are no visible signs of weathering, engravings, or additional components.
description: A heavy rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a bold striped pattern. The sides are smooth and dark, while the top alternates between light stone and recessed black grooves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running away from the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the groove depth and the alternating light and dark pattern.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or decorative obstacle
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or placed directly on a leveled surface
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and edges are sharp, indicating recent installation or low exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
